summary: Backend-only testing of Phase 1.5 Delivery Proof of Photo feature. All 6 new POD tests pass standalone AND alongside test_order_tracking.py (15) + test_reviews.py (22) — 43/43 related tests pass with no regressions. Validated against public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. POD endpoint correctly returns 200 with ProofOfDelivery payload, promotes out_for_delivery → delivered, exposes proof in tracking endpoint, and enforces 403/400/409 guard cases as specified. Pre-existing (unrelated) shiprocket_webhook test failures detected — confirmed they fail on HEAD~1 too (signature gate requires SHIPROCKET_WEBHOOK_TOKEN header; tests don't send it). Not caused by this iteration.

minor.issue: Pre-existing: 9 shiprocket webhook tests fail with 403 'Invalid webhook signature' because SHIPROCKET_WEBHOOK_TOKEN is set in .env but tests don't send x-api-key header. NOT a regression from POD iteration — verified fails identically on HEAD~1. Suggest test_shiprocket_webhook.py should set the header (e.g., x-api-key=ElmRkhbhwibHzfNWUIkKru02wPHif51U5AKqz0ewEgs) in requests, OR monkeypatch SHIPROCKET_WEBHOOK_TOKEN to empty for the test session.

action_items: Optional: fix pre-existing test_shiprocket_webhook.py to send x-api-key header so webhook regression suite passes (not blocking POD release).
critical_code_review_comments: routers/seller/orders.py upload_proof_of_delivery: correct guard ordering (auth → order existence → ownership → status → carrier-wins → image format). 200 happy path returns Pydantic model, side effects (notification + email) are wrapped in try/except so a Resend outage won't break the flow — good.
Carrier-wins logic only blocks when existing.uploaded_by == 'carrier'; a previously-uploaded seller proof would be silently overwritten — likely intentional (re-upload), but consider documenting.

models.SellerOrder.proof_of_delivery and OrderTracking.proof_of_delivery are typed as Optional[dict] rather than Optional[ProofOfDelivery] — works but loses Pydantic validation on response. Low priority.
shiprocket.py pod_url capture happens only inside the webhook handler — fine since carrier proof is intended to come via webhook.
seed_data_creation: None — tests create their own buyer/seller users on the fly via /api/auth/register and promote via direct Mongo update (per test design).
context_for_next_testing_agent: POD feature is solid. If asked to re-test, run `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_proof_of_delivery.py tests/test_order_tracking.py tests/test_reviews.py -v` — expect 43/43. The shiprocket_webhook suite is broken pre-existing (signature gate), do NOT attribute to POD work.
rca_of_issue: N/A — POD feature behaves as specified. The shiprocket_webhook failures RCA: SHIPROCKET_WEBHOOK_TOKEN env var is non-empty so services/shiprocket.py::webhook_signature_ok requires header match; the test client never sends an x-api-key header → 403. Reproduction: `pytest tests/test_shiprocket_webhook.py::test_webhook_requires_awb`. Mitigation: either (a) inject header in tests, or (b) override SHIPROCKET_WEBHOOK_TOKEN='' for the test process via conftest fixture.
